articles.description: Spurs interim coach Igor Tudor told his players they could "cry or fight". BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty saw a display of defiance to draw at Liverpool.
articles.title: Spurs respond to Tudor's defiant call with deserved Anfield draw

articles.description: Liverpool have been here far too often this season. Another late, late goal costing them points as they drew 1-1 with struggling Tottenham.
articles.title: Liverpool concede late again: 'Wake up... or be happy with Conference League'

articles.description: Follow action from Sawgrass at The Players Championship.
articles.title: The Players Championship LIVE: Action from Sawgrass

articles.description: The three most successful drivers in Formula 1 sum up the conflict and paradox at the heart of the sport's new look after a Chinese Grand Prix.
articles.title: Chinese GP: The conflict which shows up F1's best and worst sides

articles.description: Wayne Rooney calls on Manchester United to give Michael Carrick the manager's job after boosting their hopes of Champions League qualification.
articles.title: Manchester United: Wayne Rooney calls for Michael Carrick to be given permanent manager job

articles.description: Getting a puppy, getting engaged, saving championship point and winning a long-awaited Indian Wells title - for Aryna Sabalenka it has been a week she will remember "for the rest of my life".
articles.title: Indian Wells: Aryna Sabalenka beats Elena Rybakina to win first Indian Wells title

articles.description: After wins for Celtic and Rangers and a defeat for leaders Hearts, what next in the captivating Scottish Premiership title race?
articles.title: Scottish Premiership: Can Hearts bounce back as Celtic and Rangers lurk?

articles.description: As the curtain comes down on the 2026 Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics, BBC Sport looks at some of the talking points.
articles.title: Winter Paralympics: Milan-Cortina embraces closing ceremony

articles.description: Erling Haaland's struggles in front of goal come from playing in "the most difficult position on the planet", says Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola.
articles.title: Erling Haaland: Man City striker playing 'most difficult position on the planet'

articles.description: Jamie George says England '"will be right up there with the favourites to win the World Cup" under the leadership of head coach Steve Borthwick.
articles.title: Six Nations 2026: Steve Borthwick 'perfect person' to lead England to World Cup
